Questions.QuestionString: 1.              How many types are the CPIOM hardware there? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Two types: Type H and type J.
b.              Seven types: H3, H4, H6, J1, J2, J5, J7.
c.              Only one type CPIOM.

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              Which CPIOMs can be interchangeable with no core software loading necessary ? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The same type CPIOMs.
b.              The same group CPIOMs.
c.              The same type or the same group CPIOMs.

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              How many types are the CRDCs there? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Two types: Type H and type J.
b.              Only one type.
c.              Two types: Type A and type B.

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              Which CRDCs can be interchangeable with no core software loading necessary ? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The same type CRDCs.
b.              The same group CRDCs.
c.              The same type and the same group CRDCs.

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              The AFDX network is divided into: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Two identical networks:  Network A and  Network B.
b.              Only one network.
c.              Network A and  Network B, they are not identical.

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              To upload software to the CPIOMs through the Data Loading and Configuration System (DLCS), you must follow the sequence: (E&A Repair).
Answers: a.               Core software, configuration table software, RB configuration-table software, avionics application(s), database(s) if applicable.
b.              Configuration table software, core software, and RB configuration-table software, avionics application(s), database(s) if applicable.
c.              No specific sequence.

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              If you remove/install all the AFDX switches and then upload the software related to these switches, you must do the upload in three steps, in the sequence that follows: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               AFDX switches 09 and 19, AFDX switches 01, 02, 03, 04, 11, 12, 13 and 14 3,then AFDX switches 05, 06, 15 and 16.
b.              AFDX switches network A then AFDX switches network B.
c.              No specific sequence.

Questions.QuestionString: 8.               When you upload the software to each AFDX switch, you must: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               first upload the operational program software, then when the uploading is completed, upload the configuration table software.
b.              first upload the configuration table software, then when the uploading is completed, upload the operational program software.
c.              No specific sequence.

Questions.QuestionString: 9.              When uploading the software to AFDX switch If the “NO RESPONSE FROM TARGET” error message is shown at the end of the uploading of the operational program software : (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              You must reset related AFDX switch.
b.               you can ignore it and continue the procedure.
c.              You must restart DLCS in BackUp menu.

Questions.QuestionString: 10.            When you upload software to all the CRDCs, you must do: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the uploading CRDC type A first then CRDC type B.
b.               the uploading of each CRDC one at a time.
c.              the uploading CRDC type B first then CRDC type A.

Questions.QuestionString: 11.            All the LRU without AFDX capability exchange data with an AFDX subscriber through: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              CRDC
b.              CPIOM
c.              CRDC or CPIOM.

Questions.QuestionString: 12.           The CPIOMs software that are field_loadable are one core software, one resource BITE configuration table and: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              One configuration table.
b.              System software.
c.              One configuration table and System software.

Questions.QuestionString: 13.            Where are the CPIOMs installed in? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Main avionics compartment.
b.              Aft avionics Rack.
c.              Main avionics compartment  and the aft avionics Rack.

Questions.QuestionString: 14.            How to two A/C system applications hosted in the same CPIOM communicate with each other ? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              must do it through the AFDX network.
b.              do it through the internal Bus inside CPIOM.
c.              They can not communicate with each other.

Questions.QuestionString: 15.           The interface between the active CIDS director and the passenger related functions is done: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type B.
b.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type A.
c.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type A and type B.

Questions.QuestionString: 16.           The DEUs-A are connected to the directors through: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               a top line data-bus.
b.              a middle line data-bus.
c.              Enthernet bus.

Questions.QuestionString: 17.           Which gives the address for the location of the DEU-A: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              a coding switch in DEU-A.
b.              a connection box which has a coding switch.
c.              the address for the location of the DEU-A is done through software.

Questions.QuestionString: 18.           The interface between the active CIDS director and the cabin crew related functions is done: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type B.
b.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type A.
c.              through the Decoder/Encoder Units (DEUs) type A and type B.

Questions.QuestionString: 19.           The DEUs-B are connected to the directors through: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               a top line data-bus.
b.              a middle line data-bus.
c.              Enthernet bus.

Questions.QuestionString: 20.           The cabin crew related functions is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Area Call Panels (ACPs), Attendant Indication Panels (AIPs), optional AAPs, handsets, and lavatory signs.
b.              Area Call Panels (ACPs), Attendant Indication Panels (AIPs), FAP, handsets, and lavatory signs.
c.              all the Passenger Service Units (PSUs) functions (PAX individual lighting and buttons, PAX signs and calls, and loudspeakers).

Questions.QuestionString: 21.           The Cabin Assignment Module (CAM), which: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              contains the customized configuration data of the modified cabin layouts.
b.              stores prerecorded announcements, boarding music audio and announcement audio files.
c.              the software and configuration data.

Questions.QuestionString: 22.           The cabin interphone is used for the communication between the cabin crew stations and: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the cockpit and the cabin crew stations.
b.              the cockpit and the cabin crew stations, the service areas and cabin crew stations.
c.              the service areas and cabin crew stations.

Questions.QuestionString: 23.           From the cockpit, the interphone communications are possible through the cockpit handset: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the cockpit acoustic devices (boomset, microphone and boomset of the oxygen mask).
b.              the cockpit call panel, the cockpit acoustic devices (boomset, microphone and boomset of the oxygen mask).
c.              the cockpit call panel.

Questions.QuestionString: 24.           The service interphone system is used for the communication between the service areas, : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               the cockpit and service areas, cabin crew stations.
b.              the cockpit and service areas.
c.              the cockpit and service areas, the service areas and cabin crew stations.

Questions.QuestionString: 25.           The emergency EVAC signaling-system is active from: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the cockpit
b.              from the cabin control panels
c.              the cockpit or from the cabin control panels

Questions.QuestionString: 26.           How to start the Cockpit Door Surveillance System (CDSS)? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Automatically started if someone tries to go into the cockpit with the keypad of the CDLS or from the privacy door locking system (optional).
b.              Manual activation, the cockpit crew uses the VIDEO key on the.

c.              automatically started if someone tries to go into the cockpit with the keypad of the CDLS or from the privacy door locking system (optional) and manual activation, the cockpit crew uses the VIDEO key on the.

Questions.QuestionString: 27.            In case the FAP is inoperative or the FAP screen is frozen, you can do a manual reset of the FAP by: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Push and release the reset button (pinhole)  with the  TOOL-MANUAL RELEASE or with an applicable pin.
b.              Hard key FAP RESET on FAP.
c.              Manual reset of the FAP is impossible.

Questions.QuestionString: 1.              The Flight Guidance and Envelope System (FGES) is hosted in each (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Primary flight control Computer (PRIM)
b.              SECONDARY FLIGHT CONTROL COMPUTER
c.              FMS

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              The FGES functions related to the A/C control are as follows: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Autopilot (AP), Flight Director (FD), Autothrust (A/THR).
b.              Flight Envelope (FE), Autopilot (AP), Flight Director (FD), Autothrust (A/THR).
c.              Flight Envelope (FE).

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              The Computation of the characteristic speed is function of(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Autopilot (AP)
b.              Flight Director (FD)
c.              Flight Envelope (FE).

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              The computation order of the position for the control surfaces on the three axes (pitch, roll, yaw) is function: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Flight Director (FD)
b.              Autopilot (AP)
c.              Flight Envelope (FE).

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              The functions give guidance orders used in manual control mode belong to(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Flight Director (FD)
b.              Autopilot (AP)
c.              Flight Envelope (FE).

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              Which part of FGES controls the engine-thrust target N1 through the Propulsion Control System (PCS) system related to the position of the throttle control levers(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Autopilot (AP)
b.              Flight Envelope (FE).
c.              Autothrust(ATHR)

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              How to transmit the auto-flight orders of the flight crew if there is a failure of a FCU or EFIS control panel? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The auto flight system will be deactivated.
b.              There are two EFIS/FCU backup applications hosted in two CPIOMs of type J.
c.              Flight crew can use FCU back up panel on the overhead panel

Questions.QuestionString: 8.              In case of the AP is engaged(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Only the Side Stick Unit (SSU) is locked
b.              Only the rudder pedals is locked
c.              The Side Stick Unit (SSU) and the rudder pedals are locked

Questions.QuestionString: 9.              At each TCAS resolution advisory, the A/THR(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Engages if it is not already engaged
b.              Engages and set to the maximum possible in the requested direction.
c.              Stay at current status as before TCAS RA active.

Questions.QuestionString: 10.           The crew can manually disengage the A/THR as follows. He can: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Push one of the two A/THR instinctive disconnect P/BSW on the throttle control levers or push the A/THR key on the FCU or select the A/THR function on the AFS CP BACKUP page of the MFD
b.              Set the throttle control levers to the idle position or set the two throttle control-levers to the reverse position
c.              Both a and b.

Questions.QuestionString: 11.           Flight Management function is belong to (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              PRIM
b.              FMC
c.              MFD

Questions.QuestionString: 12.           The flight plan, PERF data and software P/Ns data display on(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              ND
b.              PFD
c.              MFD

Questions.QuestionString: 13.           Which mode the dual AP engagement is possible(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The Approach (APPR) and Go around (GA) modes
b.              after take off 5 second
c.              during cruise

Questions.QuestionString: 14.           When an AP is engaged: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The side sticks are locked,
b.              The rudder pedals are locked.
c.              Both a and b

Questions.QuestionString: 15.           Which of conditions the A/THR is disengaged? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              One of the two A/THR instinctive disconnect pushbutton-switches located on the throttle control levers (engines 1 or 2) is pushed
b.              The throttle control levers are set to the IDLE position.
c.              A or B are corrected

Questions.QuestionString: 16.           In case of ATHR engaged but not active(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              it manages the thrust
b.              the cockpit crews control the thrust through the throttle control levers
c.              the thrust of engine limit at idle position

Questions.QuestionString: 17.           If an FCU fault is detected (AFS or EFIS control panel fault), the related FCU backup part (AUTOFLIGHT or EFIS page on the MFD) (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              become active mode by pilot thought p/b on the overhead panel
b.              automatically changes back to the active mode
c.              become active mode by pilot thought MFP virtual p/b.

Questions.QuestionString: 18.           The FMS operates in different modes(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Dual, Independent, Single
b.              Dual, Independent and Standby
c.              Dual, Independent, Single and Standby

Questions.QuestionString: 19.           The HF and VHF communications use the HF and VHF radio frequencies to transmit the data to and from the ground facilities (airline and air traffic control) or another A/C. These data are: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Data transmitted and received through the Avionics Communication Routing System (ACRS) for data link functions
b.              Voice communications transmitted and received through the Radio and Audio Integrating Management System (RAIMS)
c.              Both a and b

Questions.QuestionString: 20.           The HF system installed on the A/C is used for(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the long-range voice and data communications (1600 nm)
b.              the short-range voice and data communications (250 nm)
c.              Data transmission to and from ACRS only.

Questions.QuestionString: 21.           The VHF system installed on the A/C is used for(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the long-range voice and data communications (1600 nm)
b.              the short-range voice and data communications (250 nm)
c.              Data transmission to and from ACRS only.

Questions.QuestionString: 22.           Where is location of High Frequency Data Radio (HFDR) transceivers? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              in the avionics compartment.
b.              In the pressurized area near the vertical stabilizer
c.              the lower front part of the vertical stabilizer section.

Questions.QuestionString: 23.           Where is location of High Frequency Data Radio (HFDR) couplers? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              in the avionics compartment.
b.              In the pressurized area near the vertical stabilizer
c.              the lower front part of the vertical stabilizer section.

Questions.QuestionString: 24.           Where is location of High Frequency Data Radio (HFDR) antenna? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              in the avionics compartment.
b.              In the pressurized area near the vertical stabilizer
c.              the lower front part of the vertical stabilizer section.

Questions.QuestionString: 25.            Which VHF system using for data transmission in normally? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              VHF 1
b.              VHF 2
c.              VHF 3

Questions.QuestionString: 26.           The VHF system includes(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Three identical Multiple VHF Data Radio (MVDR) units and three identical antennas
b.              Two identical Multiple VHF Data Radio (MVDR) units and three identical antennas
c.              Two identical Multiple VHF Data Radio (MVDR) units and two identical antennas

Questions.QuestionString: 27.           In normal operation: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The MVDR 1 COM A transceiver controls the VHF 1 channel and uses VHF antenna 1
b.              The MVDR 2 COM A transceiver controls the VHF 2 channel and uses VHF antenna 2
c.              Both A and B are correct.

Questions.QuestionString: 28.           In normal operation: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The MVDR 2 COM B transceiver controls the VHF 3 channel and uses VHF antenna 3
b.              The MVDR 1 COM B transceiver controls the VHF 3 channel and uses VHF antenna 3
c.              Both A and B are correct.

Questions.QuestionString: 29.           The HFDR couplers are used for(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              change the HF modulated signals from the A/C HFDR antenna into audio or data signals and vice versa
b.              impedance matching between the A/C HFDR antenna and the HFDR transceivers
c.              separation of the audio signal and the Selective Calling (SELCAL) code encoded in the HF modulated signal.

Questions.QuestionString: 30.           The HFDR transceivers used for: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              impedance matching between the A/C HFDR antenna and the HFDR transceivers
b.              change the HF modulated signals from the A/C HFDR antenna into audio or data signals and vice versa
c.              Both a and B

Questions.QuestionString: 31.           The HFDR transceivers used for: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              change the HF modulated signals from the A/C HFDR antenna into audio or data signals and vice versa
b.              separation of the audio signal and the Selective Calling (SELCAL) code encoded in the HF modulated signal.
c.              Both a and b

Questions.QuestionString: 32.           The HF data transmission is automatically inhibited(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              on ground by the LGERS ground signal
b.              during flight
c.              during engine start

Questions.QuestionString: 33.           The HF transmission in voice and data mode is inhibited(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              During flight
b.              on ground during fuel operations (refuel, defuel, transfer operations) through a specific signal from the Fuel Quantity and Management System (FQMS).
c.              During engine start

Questions.QuestionString: 34.           How to make overrides inhibition of HF data transmission on ground? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              By select the data transmission override on OMT
b.              Can not override this inhibition
c.              By press the GND HF DATALINK Push button

Questions.QuestionString: 35.           If there is a failure of one serviceable VHF transceiver(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Flight crews can manually switching to hot spare transceiver.
b.              an automatic reconfiguration occurs on the hot spare transceiver.
c.              All VHF connection will be lost

Questions.QuestionString: 36.           if there is a failure of the VHF 1 channel (because of the MVDR 1 COM A transceiver failure) (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              VHF 1 will be automatically reconfigured on the MVDR 1 COM B channel
b.              The VHF 3 channel will be automatically reconfigured on the MVDR 2 COM B transceiver.
c.              Both A and B are correct.

Questions.QuestionString: 37.           If there is a failure of the VHF 2 channel (because of the MVDR 2 COM A failure) (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              VHF 2 will be automatically reconfigured on the hot spare MVDR 2 COM B channel
b.              VHF 2 will be automatically reconfigured on the MVDR 1 COM B channel
c.              Flight crews can manually switching to hot spare transceiver.

Questions.QuestionString: 38.           if there is a failure of VHF antenna 3(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the VHF 1 channel (ensured by the MVDR 1 COM A transceiver) and the VHF 3 channel (ensured by the MVDR 1 COM B transceiver) share VHF antenna 1
b.              the VHF 3 channel (ensured by the MVDR 1 COM B transceiver) share VHF antenna 2
c.              the VHF 3 channel will be inoperative.

Questions.QuestionString: 39.           How to logs on the SATCOM system(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              It automatically logs on when the A/C electrical network is energized with the A/C position available from the Air Data/Inertial Reference System (ADIRS) and if the A/C is under the satellite network coverage
b.              it can be manually logged on through the selection of the log-on command on the related SATCOM menu page on the Radio Management Panel (RMP).
c.              Both A and B are correct

Questions.QuestionString: 40.           The Avionics Communication Routing System (ACRS) is used for(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The routing of the air/ground data through the Aircraft Communication Addressing and Reporting System (ACARS) network.
b.              the Internet Protocol (IP) network
c.              both a and B

Questions.QuestionString: 41.           The WACS can be stopped manually via the(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Gatelink p/b on the NLG area
b.              GATELINK P/B on the cockpit overhead panel
c.              Gatelink p/b on the passenger door 1L

Questions.QuestionString: 42.           The WACS gives communication between the aircraft and the airport network via the(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Cellular, Wifi Wireless connection and a wired gatelink connection
b.              Cellular, Wifi Wireless connection
c.              Wifi Wireless connection and a wired gatelink connection.

Questions.QuestionString: 43.           The primary components of the RAIMS (Radio and Audio Integrating Management System) are: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              One Audio Management Units (AMUs), Three Radio and Audio Management Panels (RMPs)
b.              Two Audio Management Units (AMUs), two Radio and Audio Management Panels (RMPs)
c.              Two Audio Management Units (AMUs), Three Radio and Audio Management Panels (RMPs)

Questions.QuestionString: 44.           Which of following sentence is correct? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The AMU1 is for the CAPT and F/O
b.              The AMU1 is for the CAPT and 3rd Occupant
c.              The AMU1 is for the F/O and 3rd Occupant

Questions.QuestionString: 45.           Which of following sentence is correct? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The AMU2 is for the CAPT and F/O
b.              The AMU2 is for the CAPT and 3rd Occupant
c.              The AMU2 is for the F/O and 4rd Occupant

Questions.QuestionString: 46.           In normal configuration: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The RMP1 is used by the CAPT,  The RMP2 is used by the F/O
b.              The RMP1 is used by the CAPT,  The RMP2 is used by the 3rd Occupant
c.              The RMP1 is used by the F/O,  The RMP3 is used by the 3rd Occupant

Questions.QuestionString: 47.           if there is an RMP failure(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The flight crew must manually switch by pushbutton on the overhead panel.
b.              The automatic reconfiguration in the audio management system will be done by AMU
c.              The AMU connect to these RMP will become inoperative

Questions.QuestionString: 48.           If there is a failure of the Radio Management Panel 1 and 3, the following occurs(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Only radio transceivers number 2 is working
b.              RMP 2 will control all the HF transceivers
c.              RMP 2 can control all the radio transceivers

Questions.QuestionString: 49.           The Cockpit Voice Recorder System(CVRS) connect to the(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              AMU 1
b.              AMU 2
c.              Both AMU

Questions.QuestionString: 50.           The aircraft SELCAL code can be modified via the(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              SELCAL CODE panel on the avionic bay
b.              Onboard Maintenance System(OMS)
c.              SELCAL code panel on the overhead panel

Questions.QuestionString: 51.           In normal configuration, VHF 1 can be tuned from(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              RMP 1 only
b.              RMP 2 only
c.              any RMPs with the RMP intercommunication bus

Questions.QuestionString: 52.           If a ground mechanic calls the cockpit from the GSP(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              an indication is shown on the captain RMP and the FWS triggers the buzzer
b.              an indication is shown on the three RMPs and the FWS triggers the buzzer
c.              an indication is shown on the F/O RMP and the FWS triggers the buzzer

Questions.QuestionString: 53.           What happen when the flight crews press the MECH p/b on the CALLS panel? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the horn at the NLG area  will be activated
b.              a specific indication on the GSP comes into view
c.              both a and b

Questions.QuestionString: 54.           The flight crew can show the TACS image(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              On the SD or on the NDs
b.              On the SD or on the PFDs.
c.              On the SD only

Questions.QuestionString: 55.           The TAXI keys located on the EFIS control panels allow the flight crew(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              to show the TACS video image on the NDs.
b.              to show the TACS video image on the SDs.
c.              to show the TACS video image on the PFDs.

Questions.QuestionString: 56.           The VIDEO key installed on the ECAM control panel allows the flight crew(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              to show the TACS video image on the NDs.
b.              to show the TACS video image on the SDs.
c.              to show the TACS video image on the PFDs.

Questions.QuestionString: 57.           On the PFD displays (CAPT, F/O), the TACS video images are available on the CDS when: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The A/C is on ground
b.              The A/C is in flight
c.              The A/C is on ground and the ground speed is not more than 60 kts

Questions.QuestionString: 58.           The TACs image can be displayed on the SD(E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              on ground and in flight
b.              on ground only
c.              in flight only

Questions.QuestionString: 59.           The Cockpit Voice Recording System (CVRS) records in the Solid State Cockpit Voice Recorder (SSCVR): (ME Repair, E&A Repair)

Questions.QuestionString: 33.           On ground, the Probe anti-icing function is automatically operated when: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              At least one engine is running.
b.              From ADIRS, when the Ground Speed (GS) is more than 30 kts.
c.              At least one engine is running OR from ADIRS, when the Ground Speed (GS) is more than 30 kts.

Questions.QuestionString: 34.           The alignment is the initialization mode for the IR part of the ADIRU. This mode operates : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              with IRs pushbutton are pushed.
b.              with ADIRS pushbutton switches at NAV position.
c.              on ground only with a motionless condition.

Questions.QuestionString: 35.           The standby navigation system has one standby magnetic compass, one ISIS unit (R/H ISIS unit is optional), and:(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              one standby pitot probe,  one standby static probes.
b.              one standby pitot probe, two standby static probes.
c.              two standby pitot probe, one standby static probes.

Questions.QuestionString: 36.           In automatic mode, the de-icing function of the standby probes are active if, in flight condition, the ISIS indicator detects a computed airspeed more than 50 kts or a ground speed more than 50 kts, or (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              If ADIRU1 detects one engine in operation, or a ground speed more than 30 kts.
b.              If ADIRU1 or ADIRU3 detects one engine in operation or a ground speed more than 30 kts.
c.              If ADIRUs detects one engine in operation, or a ground speed more than 30 kts.

Questions.QuestionString: 37.           The standby pitot probe and standby static probes are: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Connected with pneumatic line to ISIS.
b.              connected to the ISIS through ARINC 429 buses.
c.              Connected to the ISIS through CRDC.

Questions.QuestionString: 38.           The Multi Mode Receiver (MMR) system has the following functions: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) for navigation function support and the Landing System (LS).
b.              The Global Positioning System (GPS) for navigation function support and the Landing System (LS).
c.              Only The Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) for navigation function support.

Questions.QuestionString: 39.           The data computed by GNSS and LS can be show on the display units of the CDS and: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the Integrated Standby Instrument System (ISIS), the optional Head-Up Display (HUD).
b.              the Integrated Standby Instrument System (ISIS).
c.              the optional Head-Up Display (HUD).

Questions.QuestionString: 40.           The data computed by GNSS includes the position,  the altitude, the time (Universal Time Coordinated (UTC)), : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the ground speed.
b.              the air speed.
c.              the ground speed or the air speed.

Questions.QuestionString: 41.           The MMRs receive the satellite navigation data and signals through: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Two GNSS antennas: The GNSS antenna 1 is connected to MMR 1 and the GNSS antenna 2 is connected to the MMR 2.
b.              One GNSS antenna is connected to MMR 1 and MMR 2.
c.              Two GNSS antennas: The GNSS antenna 1 is active and the GNSS antenna 2 is standby.

Questions.QuestionString: 42.           How is the GNSS data (true track angle, ground speed, A/C position) sent to the ISIS ? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              MMR1 to ISIS-1 and MMR2 to ISIS-2 through ARINC 429 buses.
b.              Through ARINC 429 buses, MMR1sent data to ISIS and in case MMR1 lost, MMR2 sent to ISIS.
c.              MMR1 to ISIS through ARINC 429 buses, the MMR 2 is not interfaced with the ISIS.

Questions.QuestionString: 43.            An Airport Navigation Function (ANF) application which is a part of the CDS core software, which: (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              operates in all the DUs.
b.              only operates in the CAPT and F/O outer DUs.
c.              only operates in the CAPT and F/O inner DUs.

Questions.QuestionString: 44.           What happen when the outer CAPT DU is unserviceable? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the OANS image is not shown on the CAPT inner DU.
b.              the OANS image is not shown on the CAPT and F/O inner DUs.
c.              The ANF application in the F/O operates and the OANS image is still shown on the CAPT inner DU.

Questions.QuestionString: 45.            The RA system is made of: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              three subsystems. Each subsystem includes: one transceiver, one transmission antenna, one reception antenna.
b.              two subsystems. Each subsystem includes: one transceiver, one transmission antenna, one reception antenna.
c.              three subsystems. Each subsystem includes: one transceiver, one dual antenna for transmission and reception.

Questions.QuestionString: 46.            When you remove the ISIS Indicator, you must: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Open the C/B of the pitot and static standby probes before Open C/B of ISIS indicator.
b.              Open C/B of ISIS indicator before Open the C/B of the pitot and static standby probes.
c.              The sequence open C/B is not important.

Questions.QuestionString: 47.           The BITE test of the ISIS indicator (system test) can be performed: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              through a maintenance terminal.
b.              through a maintenance terminal or through the controls on its front face.
c.              through the controls on its front face, through a maintenance terminal is not mature.

Questions.QuestionString: 48.            The CAPT and/or F/O inner DU show(s) an OANS image when On the CAPT and/or F/O EFIS control panel(s): (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The display mode selector-switch to NAV or ARC and the range selector switch to ZOOM.
b.              The Range the range selector switch to ZOOM whatever the display mode selector-switch position.
c.              The display mode selector-switch to NAV, ARC or PLAN and the range selector switch to ZOOM.

Questions.QuestionString: 49.            The ON BAT indicator in ADIRS Mode Selector Unit comes on when? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              One or more ADIRUs are supplied by batteries.
b.              You align ADIRUs with only batteries supplied.
c.              Battery is not be able to supply the ADIRUs.

Questions.QuestionString: 50.            In case of loss of AIR DATA parameters on CAPT or F/O PFD and ND, the affected displays can show  ADR 3 DATA : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Automatically
b.              By set AIR DATA selector switch.
c.              By set  ATT HDG selector switch

Questions.QuestionString: 51.            When IR or ADR pushbutton on ADIRS control panel set to OFF: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              IR or ADR part is not power supplied.
b.              The ADIRU stop to compute and transmit the ADR or IR data to the aircraft systems.
c.              The ADIRU continues to compute ADR or IR data but does not transmit them to the aircraft systems.

Questions.QuestionString: 52.            In normal configuration, the two AIR DATA and ATT HDG selector switches are at NORM: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               The Captain (CAPT) PFD and ND show ADR1 and IR1 data - The First Officer (F/O) PFD and ND show ADR2 and IR2 data.
b.              The Captain (CAPT) PFD and ND show ADR1 and IR2 data - The First Officer (F/O) PFD and ND show ADR2 and IR1 data.
c.              The CAPT PFD, CAPT ND, F/O PFD, F/O ND show ADR1 and IR1 data and - The ADR2 and IR2 data is backup.

Questions.QuestionString: 53.            Each ADIRU sends air data and inertial data through the Avionics Full Duplex Switched Ethernet (AFDX) network to each Display Unit (DU) of the CDS. As a backup solution,: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              it also sends this data through an ARINC 429 bus to the Captain (First Officer) outer and inner DUs.
b.              ADIRU3 sends this data through an ARINC 429 bus to the Captain (First Officer) outer and inner DUs.
c.              ADIRU1 (ADIRU2) also sends this data through an ARINC 429 bus to the Captain (First Officer) outer and inner DUs.

Questions.QuestionString: 54.            Where are the radio height data displays? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              PFDs.
b.              NDs.
c.              PFDs and NDs.

Questions.QuestionString: 55.            In Normal operation, the radio height data display on CDS is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the median value between RA1, RA2 and RA3.
b.              RA1 for CAPT side, RA2 for F/O side and RA3 is backup.
c.              RA1 for CAPT PFD and F/O ND, RA2 for CAPT ND and F/O PFD, RA3 is backup.

Questions.QuestionString: 56.            When only two RA transceivers operates: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              one RA value is shown on the CAPT side and the other RA value is shown on the F/O side.
b.              the radio height data display on CDS is the median value between remain two RAs.
c.              the radio height data display on ND is lost.

Questions.QuestionString: 57.           When only one RA transceivers operates: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the radio height data display on F/O side is lost.
b.              each PFD shows the available radio height
c.              each ND shows the available radio height.

Questions.QuestionString: 58.           Where are the RA transmission and reception antennas installed? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              at the bottom of the forward fuselage
b.              at the bottom of the rearward fuselage
c.              RA1 and RA2 antennas at the bottom of the forward fuselage, RA3 antennas at the bottom of the rearward fuselage.

Questions.QuestionString: 59.            In the backup mode, the VOR frequency tuning and VOR course data can be: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Tuning on RMP1, RMP2, or RMP3 through the NAV menu.
b.              Tuning on RMP1 for VOR1, RPM2 for VOR2 through the NAV menu.
c.              Tuning on RMP1 or RMP2 through the NAV menu.

Questions.QuestionString: 60.            In normal mode, the DME interrogators receive the frequency can be tuning: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Automatically.
b.              Manually.
c.              Automatically and manually.

Questions.QuestionString: 61.            Where the DME data can be display? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              PFDs.
b.              NDs.
c.              PFDs and NDs.

Questions.QuestionString: 62.            The DME data display on the ND when: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The VOR or VOR-D key on the EFIS control panel is pushed.
b.              The mode selector switch on the EFIS control panel is set to ROSE or ARC.
c.              A and B.

Questions.QuestionString: 63.           The Aircraft Environment Surveillance System (AESS) has the mode of operation: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Normal mode, mixed mode, downgraded mode and automatic reconfiguration mode.
b.              normal mode, mixed mode, and automatic reconfiguration mode.
c.              normal mode, mixed mode, and downgraded mode .

Questions.QuestionString: 64.           Which is the Master AESU? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The AESU with the WXR/TAWS function group activated.
b.              The AESU with the TCAS/XPDR function group activated.
c.              Always is the AESU1.

Questions.QuestionString: 65.           The WXR/PWS function detects and gives the location of the atmospheric disturbances in the area defined by the antenna scanning: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Plus or minus 90 Deg. in azimuth and Plus or minus 10 Deg. in tilt.
b.              Plus or minus 90 Deg. in azimuth and Plus or minus 15 Deg. in tilt.
c.              Plus or minus 80 Deg. in azimuth and Plus or minus 15 Deg. in tilt.

Questions.QuestionString: 66.           The weather radar image show on the CAPT (F/O) ND when: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The WX key on CAPT (F/O) EFIS control panel is pushed and he ND display mode is set to VOR, ILS, NAV or ARC mode.
b.              Select SYS1 (SYS2) WXR/TAWS on the AESS control panel.
c.              The WX key on CAPT (F/O) EFIS control panel is pushed

Questions.QuestionString: 67.            To do the uploading of software to the Aircraft Environment Surveillance Unit (AESU), you must: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Complete the uploading IOM software component before upload the other AESU software components.
b.              You can upload all AESU software components at the time.
c.              No specified sequence.

Questions.QuestionString: 68.             To do the uploading of the weather radar software: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              You must select the PN of weather radar software and also PN of the RADAR TOPOGRAPHY DATABASE software.
b.              You must select the PN of weather radar software and also PN of the PWS ENVMOD software.
c.              Complete the uploading WEATHER RADAR  software components before upload the other software components.

Questions.QuestionString: 69.            During the Uploading of software to the AESU,  pop-up message “ Upload aborted by DLCS” (Effect code 4546K013) come into view, you must: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Do a Reset of OSFC by reset switch on RESET panel.
b.              Go to Back up menu and restart DLCS application.
c.              Do a reset of the AESU.

Questions.QuestionString: 70.           What do you do when the OFF position of one ADIRS selector switch is inoperative? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Must deactivate related ADIRS selector.
b.              Must deactivate related ADIRU.
c.              Deferred IAW with MEL with no (M) procedure.

Questions.QuestionString: 71.           Where is the ADIRU3 installed? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              In avionics compartment.
b.              In aft avionics rack.
c.              Between FWD ang AFT cargo compartment.

Questions.QuestionString: 72.            To calculate the A/C position, ADIRUs used: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              FMS data.
b.              GPS data.
c.              ILS data.

Questions.QuestionString: 73.            Each ISP provide static pressure information to related ADIRU as follows: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Directly through ARINC 429.
b.              Through CRDC.
c.              Directly though pneumatic line.

Questions.QuestionString: 74.           The angle of attach (AOA) parameter is sensed by: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              AOA sensor.
b.              AOA part of the MFP.
c.              AOA part of the MFP and additional AOA probe.

Questions.QuestionString: 75.           Manual alignment of the ADIRUs is done through: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              FMS pages display on multifunction Display.
b.              ADIRS pushbutton switches at NAV position.
c.              IRS pushbutton switches are pushed.

Questions.QuestionString: 76.            MMR transmit the following information to FGES: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Position, Time, ground speed.
b.              ILS data.
c.              Position, altitude, ground speed, time (UTC).

Questions.QuestionString: 77.           The VHF ommidirectional Range Unit 1 receives the VOR signal from: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              A dual VOR antenna.
b.              A single VOR antenna.
c.              Two single VOR antenna

Questions.QuestionString: 78.           The AESS can be controlled from the AESS control panel on pedestal and the: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              SUVR menu on the Multifunction display.
b.              SUVR menu on the OIS display.
c.              Only from the AESS control panel on pedestal.

Questions.QuestionString: 79.           The weather radar predictive winshear system includes one WXR antenna assembly and:  (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              two applications installed in two AESUs.
b.              two applications hosted in the AESU.
c.              two applications hosted in two CPIOMs.

Questions.QuestionString: 80.           The OMS applications are hosted in: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              ASFC.
b.              OSFC.
c.              ASFC and OSFC.

Questions.QuestionString: 81.           The maintenance applications are hosted in ASFC  are in charge of maintenance of A/C system and: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              OSFC.
b.              ASFC and OSFC.
c.              ASFC itself.

Questions.QuestionString: 82.           The OMS applications that are hosted in ASFC include: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              CMS-ACD, DLCS-ACD, ACMS, PDMMF, LMF, MCA.
b.              ADS, MCA, CMS- ACD, DLCS- ACD, ACMS.
c.              CMS- ACD, DLCS-ACD, ACMS, PDMMF.

Questions.QuestionString: 83.           Which OMS application computes the A/C PFR? (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              CMS- ACD.
b.              MCA.
c.              CMS- ACD and MCA.

Questions.QuestionString: 84.           The OMS applications that are hosted in OSFC include: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              ADS, SDL, LMF, MCA and the option electronic logbook.
b.              ADS, DLCS-ACD, LMF, MCA and the option electronic logbook.
c.              ADS, SDL, LMF, CMS-ACD and the option electronic logbook.

Questions.QuestionString: 85.           The data loading application used for field loadable software uploading in the OSFC is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The Simple Data Loader (SDL).
b.              DLCS-ACD.
c.              DLCS_ACD or SDL.

Questions.QuestionString: 86.           The data loading application used for ACD and ASFC software uploading is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The Simple Data Loader (SDL).
b.              DLCS-ACD.
c.              DLCS_ACD or SDL.

Questions.QuestionString: 87.           The OMS applications can be accessed from the OMT, the CAPT and F/O outer displays of CDS, : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the optional Portable Multipurpose Access Terminals (PMAT)
b.              the flight attendant panels(FAP) and the airline cabin laptop (decreased functions).
c.              a and b.

Questions.QuestionString: 88.           The CMS can be operates in: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The ground mode and the flight mode.
b.              The normal mode (on ground and in flight) and the interactive only on ground.
c.              The normal mode only in flight and the interactive only on ground.

Questions.QuestionString: 89.            The CMS-ACD application exchanges data with the avionics components through: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the SDM which operates as firewall.
b.              the SCIs which operate as firewalls.
c.              the SCIs and SDM which operate as firewalls.

Questions.QuestionString: 90.            The avionics computers and the ASCF field loadable software to be uploaded can be selected from: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The inbox area and the repository area in the field-loadable software repository hosted in the ASFC.
b.              The inbox area in the field-loadable software repository hosted in the OSFC and the repository area in the field-loadable software repository hosted in the ASFC.
c.              The inbox area and the repository area in the field-loadable software repository hosted in the OSFC.

Questions.QuestionString: 91.            After the uploading of a new software version, the previous software version is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Kept onboard a repository until configuration validation.
b.              Kept onboard a inbox.
c.              Will be erased.

Questions.QuestionString: 92.            Aircraft Condition Monitoring System included two primary applications hosted in: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Central Data Acquisition Unit (CDAU) and the ASFC.
b.              Central Data Acquisition Unit (CDAU) and the OSFC.
c.              The ASFC and the OSFC.

Questions.QuestionString: 93.            The ACMS application that acquires the A/C system parameters and computes the related reports is: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              ACMS –SA hosted in the ASFC.
b.              ACMS real time hosted in CDAU and ACMS –SA hosted in the ASFC.
c.              ACMS real time hosted in CDAU.

Questions.QuestionString: 94.            The entry into MEL is done through: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              ECAM warning.
b.              Dispatch massage.
c.              Fault code.

Questions.QuestionString: 95.           The ways to get access to the MEL to determine the dispatch condition status related to the flight crew entry: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Free access from the OMS documentation menu.
b.              Direct access to the MEL correlated entry from a dedicated hyperlink on the OMS Post Flight report (PFR) page.
c.              Free access from the OMS documentation menu or Direct access to the MEL correlated entry from a dedicated hyperlink on the OMS Post Flight report (PFR) page

Questions.QuestionString: 96.            Downloading of Files from Equipment can be done: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Through Internet Protocol (IP) Communication with the Data Export Function.
b.              Through Internet Protocol (IP) Communication OR to a USB Mass-Storage Device with the Data Export Function.
c.              Only to a USB Mass-Storage Device with the Data Export Function.

Questions.QuestionString: 97.             To know the missing software components (they are installed and validated but they stay in the Inbox repository), on the maintenance terminal, on the “MAINTENANCE HOME PAGE”, in the « Aircraft Management » pull-down menu, select: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              “S/W Repository Management”.
b.              “Upload &Conf Validation”.
c.              “A/C Config History”.

Questions.QuestionString: 98.            To Export the Aircraft Condition Monitoring System (ACMS) Data. The type of ACMS files can be: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              QAR, SAR, REP, RTM, ACMS.
b.              REP, QAR, SAR, RTM.
c.              QAR, SAR.

Questions.QuestionString: 99.            To get the “MAINTENANCE HOME PAGE” on the OIS displays, the operator must:  (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              push the Shift and OIS MENU keys on the OIS keyboard, at the same time.
b.              push the Ctrl and OIS MENU keys on the OIS keyboard, at the same time.
c.              First set the EFB/AVNCS selector switch to AVNCS, then push the Ctrl and OIS MENU keys on the OIS keyboard, at the same time.

Questions.QuestionString: 100.         To get Access to Maintenance and Flight Crew Applications through the ASF Backup Menu: On the OIS keyboard or on the OMT keyboard: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Push the Shift key and keep it pushed while you push the OIS Menu key.
b.              Push the Ctrl key and keep it pushed while you push the OIS Menu key.
c.              Push the OIS Menu key.

Questions.QuestionString: 101.        The OIS applications and related database are hosted in the equipment that follow: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The Avionics Server Function Cabinet (ASFC), the Open world Server Function Cabinet (OSFC).
b.              The Avionics Server Function Cabinet (ASFC), the Open world Server Function Cabinet (OSFC), two Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) laptops (Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) laptops).
c.              The Open world Server Function Cabinet (OSFC), two Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) laptops (Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) laptops).

Questions.QuestionString: 102.        The interface between the avionics system and the OIS is done: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              with SDM through AFDX, ARINC 429 or discrete links.
b.              with two SCIs and SDM through AFDX, ARINC 429 or discrete links
c.              with two SCIs through AFDX, ARINC 429 or discrete links

Questions.QuestionString: 103.        The ASFC is connected to the SCIs: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              only through Ethernet and discrete links.
b.              through AFDX, ARINC 429 or discrete links
c.              through Ethernet, ARINC 429 or discrete links

Questions.QuestionString: 104.        The OSFC is connected to the SCIs: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              only through Ethernet and discrete links.
b.              through AFDX, ARINC 429 or discrete links.
c.              through Ethernet, ARINC 429 or discrete links

Questions.QuestionString: 105.        The two cabinets ASFC and OSFC are connected through: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Ethernet links (with restricted communication because of the firewall function).
b.              AFDX links (with restricted communication because of the firewall function).
c.              Ethernet and ARINC 429 links (with restricted communication because of the firewall function).

Questions.QuestionString: 106.        The flight control domain has protection given by: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              the two SCIs and SDM from the intrusion of possible malicious-data that come from the ASFC.
b.              the two SCIs (firewalls) from the intrusion of possible malicious-data that come from the ASFC.
c.              SDM from the intrusion of possible malicious-data that come from the ASFC.

Questions.QuestionString: 107.        The ASFC, which has protection given by: (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.               the SDM (part of the OSFC) from the AISD possible malicious data.
b.              the two SCIs and SDM from the intrusion of possible malicious-data that come from the ASFC.
c.              the two SCIs (firewalls) from the intrusion of possible malicious-data that come from the OSFC.

Questions.QuestionString: 108.        The CAB DATA TO OIS P/BSW in OFF position: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              stops the communication from the IFE and Cabin Wireless LAN Unit (CWLU) to OIS.
b.              stops the communication from the AISD to the ACD IS.
c.              stops the wireless communication with Internet-protocol ground network.

Questions.QuestionString: 109.        The OIS DATA TO AVNCS P/BSW in OFF position: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              stops the communication from the IFE and Cabin Wireless LAN Unit (CWLU) to OIS.
b.              stops the communication from the AISD to the ACD IS.
c.              stops the wireless communication with Internet-protocol ground network.

Questions.QuestionString: 110.        The GATELINK P/BSW in OFF position: (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              stops the communication from the IFE and Cabin Wireless LAN Unit (CWLU) to OIS.
b.              stops the communication from the AISD to the ACD IS.
c.              stops the wireless communication with Internet-protocol ground network.

Questions.QuestionString: 111.        The wireless system has : (ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              The basic architecture gives a wireless access to the cabin crew.
b.              The basic architecture give a wireless connectivity to the passengers.

Questions.QuestionString: 147.        Regard to repair of optical fiber cables,  Which of the following statement(s) is(are) true? (E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Number of repairs per line:  A maximum of four repairs per line is permitted if, after each repair, you do an optical power or insertion loss measurement (ref ESPM 20-52-25) and the results agree with the values specified in the TSM or AMM for the related system(s).

b.              Number of repairs per link:  A maximum of three repairs per line is permitted if, after each repair, you do an optical power or insertion loss measurement (ref ESPM 20-52-25) and the results agree with the values specified in the TSM or AMM for the related system(s).

c.              Number of repairs per line:  A maximum of three repairs per line is permitted if, after each repair, you do an optical power or insertion loss measurement (ref ESPM 20-52-25) and the results agree with the values specified in the TSM or AMM for the related system(s).
