Questions.QuestionString: 3.              When the XBLEED selector is in the AUTO position, it will be closed automatically in case(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Both Engine Bleed Air System (EBAS) are operative in normal configuration, without APU bleed air
b.              Air is supplied from the HP ground cart
c.              No bleed air is available.

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              When all APU and ENGINEs bleed airs are available(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.               APU bleed having priority over engine bleed air.
b.               APU bleed will supply bleed air only if the X BLEED selector is selected OPEN
c.               APU bleed will supply bleed air only if the ENG BLEED pushbuttons are selected OFF

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              The HPGC supply symbol (white GND and green triangle) comes into view in ENG BLEED page only when(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The A/C is on ground (input from LGERS) and there is no APU nor ENG bleed air supply
b.              The bleed ducts are pressurized (pressure above 20 psi).
c.              All of above

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              Engine Bleed Air System (EBAS) is used for:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Bleed air pressure regulation (including HP/Intermediate Pressure (IP) transfer) and bleed air temperature regulation
b.              System monitoring and protections.
c.              All of above

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              Location of the Precooler (PCE)(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.               On the pylon
b.              Right above engine core
c.               On the wing

Questions.QuestionString: 8.              The fan air flow exiting the PCE is vented to(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The engine core zone and is subsequently vented overboard.
b.              Overboard through pylons outlets
c.              None of above

Questions.QuestionString: 9.              To replace the PCE need the dedicated tool(ME Repair)
Answers: a.              Due to the location
b.              Due to the weight
c.              Both of above

Questions.QuestionString: 10.           On the BLEED page which valve are not displayed(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              FAV and OPSOV
b.              MPV and OPSOV
c.              HPV and FAV

Questions.QuestionString: 11.           When A/C inflight, at normal configuration, which valve ensure ENGINE BLEED system pressure regulation(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Manifold Pressure Valve (MPV)
b.              HP Bleed Valve (HPV)
c.              Fan Air Valve (FAV)

Questions.QuestionString: 12.           In case of overpressure ( the MPV does not operate correctly), the ENGINE BLEED system protection is ensured by(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: a.              Backup a pneumatic protection is done by the overpressure shut-off valve
b.              Electrically controlled and pneumatically operated the  overpressure shut-off valve
c.              Electrically controlled and pneumatically operated the  fan air valve

Questions.QuestionString: 13.           When A/C inflight, at normal configuration, which valve ensure ENGINE BLEED system temperature regulation(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Manifold Pressure Valve (MPV)
b.              HP Bleed Valve (HPV)
c.              Fan Air Valve (FAV)

Questions.QuestionString: 14.           The functions of the overheat detection system (OHDS) are:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Leak detection and leak isolation
b.              Leak detection and leak location
c.              Leak detection,  leak isolation and leak location

Questions.QuestionString: 11.                How many  pressure sources for potable water pressurization ? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   Bleed air or compressed air ground cart
b.                  Bleed air or compressed air ground cart or A potable water pump
c.                   Bleed air or compressed air ground cart or electrical compressor

Questions.QuestionString: 12.                Can you work on the toilet waste system and the portable system at the same time? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   Yes, the two systems are independent.
b.                  No, it can cause the contamination to the portable water system
c.                   No, you can not use water car and waste car at the same time.

Questions.QuestionString: 13.                Can you set the water quantity at FAP at any time ?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   Only when PWSP access door  is closed
b.                  Only when PWSP access door  is opened
c.                   Only when A/C is energized and fill-mode was not started at the PWIP.

Questions.QuestionString: 14.                Which level you can preselection on FAP? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   The value in steps of 10% of the total potable-water quantity, between 30% and 100%.
b.                  The value in steps of 25% of the total potable-water quantity.
c.                   The value in steps of 20% of the total potable-water quantity.

Questions.QuestionString: 15.                When water from faucet in LAV can not stop, what should you do? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   You can disconnect the water line to the faucet and INOP the  LAV.
b.                  Let the water flow because it flow small water.
c.                   Close the SOV and deactivate the LAV.

Questions.QuestionString: 16.                When water form faucet in LAV can not stop and we close the SOV, (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Water flows from faucet stop so we can use toilet in that LAV normally.
b.              All the water supply to that LAV stop and the LAV is considered inoperative.
c.              Water stops flowing to faucet and still supply to toilet line.

Questions.QuestionString: 17.                What is aircraft configuration to refill the water tanks? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   It does not matter if the aircraft is energized or not.
b.                  You can not refill the water tanks if A/C have no electrical
c.                   You can only refill the water tanks when Preselection unit is energized

Questions.QuestionString: 18.                Which the function of potable water pump?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   Increase the pressure and circulation water in the distribution lines
b.                  Reduce time for water refill
c.                   Back up for air bleed source when it not avail

Questions.QuestionString: 19.                When does the vacuum generator operate? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   On the ground or during low-altitude flight.
b.                  Aircraft is on ground and up to 18000 ± 700 ft.
c.                   Aircraft is on ground and up to 20000± 700 ft.

Questions.QuestionString: 20.                Which type sensor for waste tank? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.                   Internal skin quantity sensor meshes
b.                  Waste level sensor is a capacitive sensor and waste level transmitter measures the hydrostatic pressure of the tank content.
c.                   Waste level sensor is monitor by the VSC and waste level transmitter is monitor by PIM.

Questions.QuestionString: 1.              The function of the Inert Gas System (IGS) is to decrease the oxygen concentration in the(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Wing tanks only.
b.              Center tank only.
c.              All the tanks.

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              The function of the Air Separation Modules (ASMs)(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Release of Oxygen Enriched Air (OEA)
b.              Release of Nitrogen Enriched Air (NEA)
c.              Collect of Nitrogen Enriched Air (NEA)

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              The air that goes into the IGGS(Inert Gas Generation System) comes from:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Ambien air.
b.              Conditioned Service Air System (CSAS).
c.              None of above.

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              The IGGS operates(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              In flight only
b.              All time
c.              On ground to test system or in flight

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              Temperature Isolation Valve(TIV)(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Electrically controlled and operated
b.              Electrically controlled and pneumatically operated
c.              Pneumatically controlled and operated

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              Which valve has mechanical visual position-indicator(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Temperature Isolation Valve(TIV)
b.              Dual Flow and Shut-Off Valve(DFSOV)
c.              Both of them

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              Where are the IGGS pallets located(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              In wing leading edges
b.              In FWD cargo
c.              In pack compartments

Questions.QuestionString: 8.              The IGGS starts to operate(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Automatically when it is available.
b.              Command by crew when it is available.
c.              Command by Fuel Quantity and Management System (FQMS) when it is available.

Questions.QuestionString: 12.               The main features of the APU HGT-1700[A] are:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             1 stage Load Compressor, 2 stage engine compressor , 3 stage engine turbine and Accessory Gearbox .
B.             1 stage Load Compressor , 3 stage engine compressor ,2 stage engine turbine and Accessory Gearbox .
C.             1 stage Load Compressor , 2 stage engine compressor, 1 engine turbine and Accessory Gearbox .

Questions.QuestionString: 13.               Where are  the APU Start Power Unit (SPU) and Starter Generator Control Unit (SGCU) located?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             In the aft cargo compartment.
B.             In the bulk cargo com partment.
C.             In the forward avionic compartment.

Questions.QuestionString: 14.               The APU is suspended in tail cone by means of:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             4 mounts.
B.             3 mounts.
C.             5 mounts.

Questions.QuestionString: 15.               The APU air intake actuator maneuvers the intake flap between how many positions?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             2: Open and Closed.
B.             3: fully Open, partially open, Closed.
C.             3: Open(open in flight), Open( open on Ground), Closed.

Questions.QuestionString: 16.               What does the ECB use to cut-off the power supply to the actuator when the air intake reaches the end positions?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             Position feed back from LVDT.
B.             Position feed back from RVDT.
C.             Position feed back from internal switches.

Questions.QuestionString: 17.               Can the air intake flap actuator operate if it has electrical failed?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             Can not.
B.             It has a manual opening lever.
C.          It has a manual  inch hex drive.

Questions.QuestionString: 18.               What power does the Starter Generator Control Unit( SGCU) manage to utilize the APU start?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             AC power from Aircraft busses.
B.             DC power from Aircraft busses.
C.             AC or DC power from Aircraft busses

Questions.QuestionString: 19.               What is true regarding the APU Starter/Generator supply?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             90 KVA , 115 VAC.
B.             120 KVA, 115 VAC.
C.             150KVA, 230 VAC.

Questions.QuestionString: 20.               The APU Starter/Generator( SG) is cooled and lubricated which is:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             Independent from other APU systems.
B.             Shared with APU oil system.
C.             Shared with APU fuel system.

Questions.QuestionString: 21.               What is the function of The APU Start Power Unit(SPU) ?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             It converts 230VAC to 115VAC voltage to supply the SGCU for APU start.
B.             It converts 28VDC to a high DC voltage to supply the SGCU for APU start.
C.             It converts 115VAC to 230VAC voltage to supply the SGCU for APU start.

Questions.QuestionString: 22.               What is the APU Starter/Generator duty cycle? (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             2 attempted starts maximum in any case, then a 1 hour cool down period is required prior to the next attempted start.
B.             3 attempted starts , a 30 second delay between them, then a 1 hour cool down period is required prior to the next attempted start.
C.             4 attempted starts totally, then trouble shooting if the last attempt is not successful.

Questions.QuestionString: 23.               What is the ECB location?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             Forward Avionic compartment.
B.             Aft cargo compartment.
C.             Rearward of bulk cargo compartment.

Questions.QuestionString: 24.               When is the ECB powered?(ME Repair, E&A Repair)
Answers: A.             The A/C is energized.
B.             The A/C is energized and Master switch “On”.
C.             The A/C is energized from the external power cart.

Questions.QuestionString: 25.               From where is the APU emergency shutdown can be manually initiated?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             Cockpit Overhead Panel, EXT PWR Receptacle, Integrated Refuel Panel.
B.             Cockpit Overhead Panel, NLG Services Panel, Integrated Refuel Panel.
C.             Cockpit Overhead Panel, NLG Services Panel, EXT PWR Receptacle.

Questions.QuestionString: 26.               What is true regarding the APU start modes?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             APU start sequence is same on ground as in flight.
B.             Auto start is initiated by ECB automatically in case of In flight Total Engine Flame Out (TEFO).
C.             APU can be auto or manual start on ground.

Questions.QuestionString: 27.               In which case does the ‘FAULT’ light illuminate on APUMaster Switch?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             In the event of a critical failure detected during the B.I.T or a protective shut down.
B.             In the event of coming fuel pressure lower than limit.
C.             In the event of anoil level lower than limit.

Questions.QuestionString: 28.               If APU Data Memory Module (DMM) shows problems :(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             You could swap DMMs between APUs for trouble shooting.
B.             You have to try with new one.
C.             You could try with used serviceable DMM.

Questions.QuestionString: 29.               What is true regarding APU maintenance?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             You could swapDMMs between APUs for trouble shooting.
B.             You could replaceDMM and ECB at the same time.
C.             You could replace DMM with new one.

Questions.QuestionString: 30.               When lube filter or SG scavenge filter is clogged:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             The APU will shutdown automatically in any case.
B.             The APU will shutdown automatically if in flight.
C.             The APU will shutdown automatically if on ground.

Questions.QuestionString: 31.               The APU oil cooler reduces the oil temperature using air:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             Supplied by cooling fan driven by gearbox.
B.             Sucked with aventuri effect generated by the exhaust gas.
C.             Tapped from outlet of Load compressor.

Questions.QuestionString: 32.               The function of the thermostat bypass valve of the APU oil system is:(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             Allowing the oil to bypass the oil cooler if oil temperature is too cold.
B.             Allowing the oil to bypass the oil cooler if it clogs.
C.             Allowing the oil to bypass the oil cooler if it clogs or oil temperature is too cold.

Questions.QuestionString: 33.               If the advisory‘OIL LVL LO’ is pulsing on APU page:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             It means low oil level condition.
B.             The APU canstill  operate for another 60hours at normal oil consumption.
C.             It means the oil level to be checked.

Questions.QuestionString: 34.               In case of high oil temperature or low oil pressure:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             The ECB will generate the maintenance message.
B.             The APU will shutdown automatically.
C.             ECB automatically aborts the start sequence.

Questions.QuestionString: 35.               How do you perform the APU manual rotation?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             By removal of IGV actuator to access the rotating shaft.
B.             By removal of the access cover of the inlet plenum to turn the rotating shaft.
C.             By removal of the vent line and the air/oil separator cover to access the rotating shaft.

Questions.QuestionString: 36.               Which components of APU are actuated with fuel medium?(ME Repair)
Answers: A.             IGV actuator, Surge Control Valve.
B.             IGV actuator, Bleed Control Valve.
C.             IGV actuator, Bleed Control Valve and Surge Control Valve.

Questions.QuestionString: 37.               The APU low fuel pressure switch is:(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             Monitored by the ECB and provide notification of low fuel pressure on the ECAM.
B.             Monitored by the ECB and providelow fuel pressure protective shutdown.
C.             Monitored by the ECB andrecorded by CMS.

Questions.QuestionString: 38.               What is true regarding the APU shutdown?(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: A.             In flight, the protective shutdowns are inhibited even in case of  major failures
B.             During emergency or protective shut down there is 60 seconds cool down period
C.             In normal shutdown the ECB decreases slowly the APU speed for a cooldown period

Questions.QuestionString: 1.              The cockpit door can be kept electrically unlocked for maintenance (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              By keeping the cockpit door toggle switch in the unlock position.
b.              By using the deadbolt to prevent door from closing.
c.              By pushing the maintenance switch on overhead panel.

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              The cockpit door strikes are immediately de-energized to unlock the door when decompression is sensed (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              By pressure sensors of ISIS system.
b.              By pressure sensors of CPCS (cabin pressure control system).
c.              By pressure sensors integrated in CDLCU (cockpit door locking control unit).

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              The status cockpit door “closed and locked”  on the cockpit door panel(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Sent from micro-switch that attached to each electrical strike.
b.              Based on voting logic of status of three electrical strikes.
c.              Sent from the switch in center strike.

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              Decompression Deceleration Device decrease the speed of the cockpit door movement(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              When cockpit door is opened rapidly.
b.              When shear pin of the device break to activate brake assembly.
c.              Both of above.

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              Cabin Decompression and Evacuation Panel integrated in cockpit door(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Will open into the direction of the cockpit when rapid cabin decompression occurred.
b.              For evacuation of the cockpit when cockpit door is blocked.
c.              Both of above.

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              The escape hatch can be (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Opened from inner side only
b.              Opened/Closed from outside and inside
c.              Opened/Closed from inner side and Opened from outside

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              The passenger door bumper (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Absorbs the movement of the door while it opens or closes.
b.              Opens the door in emergency operation.
c.              Stops the door to prevent it from crashing on A/C skin

Questions.QuestionString: 8.              The passenger door cannot be opened in flight due to(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The design profile of guide roller brackets and door stop fittings to prevent the relative movement between guide rollers and guide roller brackets as well as door stop fittings and door frame stop fittings under cabin pressure.
b.              Under cabin pressure the guide roller brackets lock the guide roller from moving away.
c.              Under cabin pressure the door stop fittings cannot move away from door frame stop fittings.

Questions.QuestionString: 9.              When the door moved away from the door-frame(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The inner and outer handles cannot operate due to the anti-lowering lever fully locks the internal door mechanism.
b.              The inner handle can be operated.
c.              The slide selector lever can be operated.

Questions.QuestionString: 10.           When the Slide Selector Lever moves to the DISARMED position(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The girt bar disengages from the floor fittings.
b.              The girt bar stays connected to the fuselage floor fittings.
c.              The girt bar disengage from the slide pack.

Questions.QuestionString: 11.           Checking the DEOA (Damper and Emergency Operation  Actuator) serviceable by(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Checking a pressure gauge installed on the cylinder in green band.
b.              Checking a pressure gauge installed on the cylinder and checking indication on FAP.
c.              Checking the bottle pressure status on FAP.

Questions.QuestionString: 12.           After the passenger door open in emergency mode(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The door can be closed after acting on safety exhaust plug to release pressure in  DEOA (Damper and Emergency Operation  Actuator).
b.              The DEOA can be charged with gas on wing for serviceability.
c.              Both of above

Questions.QuestionString: 13.           The latched and locked of cargo door is checking by(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Eight locking cam windows show green painted surface.
b.              An aft hook indicator flag does not protrude.
c.              Both of above.

Questions.QuestionString: 14.           The EMP run automatically (ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              When the cargo door is unlatched, the EMP run 30s for bleeding hydraulic.
b.              When door unlatched and the toggle switch on the CDOP (cargo door operation panel) moves away from "NEUTRAL/DOOR STOP" position.
c.              Both of above.

Questions.QuestionString: 15.           At starting of cargo door normal closure(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Hydraulic pressure is sent to the actuator extension chamber.
b.              Hydraulic pressure is sent to both chambers of actuator to prevent the free fall of cargo door.
c.              Hydraulic pressure is sent to actuator retraction chamber.

Questions.QuestionString: 16.           For cargo door operation, if EMP( electric motor pump) fail to run automatically(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Always two staffs need for cargo door operation, one at cargo door operation panel, one at hydraulic yellow service panel to pressurize system.
b.              One staff at cargo door operation panel is enough, you can start EMP manual from cockpit.

c.              Cargo door can only be opened manually with anHydraulic Auxiliary Pump (HAP) through a gearbox and with the aid ofa hand pump handle installed in two parts (crank and extension lever) onthe two hydraulic Ground Service Panels (GSPs) (Yellow and Green).

Questions.QuestionString: 17.           The red warning light on DIP( door indication panel) will flash(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              When the A/C on ground and pressure inside A/C is 2.5 mbar higher than pressure outside.
b.              When the A/C on ground, pressure inside A/C is 2.5 mbar higher than pressure outside, the slide lever is in the disarmed position.
c.              When the A/C on ground, parking brake on, pressure inside A/C 2.5 mbar higher than pressure outside, the slide lever is in the disarmed position.

Questions.QuestionString: 18.           When CPCS dose not get the closed and latched signal from bulk cargo door(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The pressurization of A/C is inhibited.
b.              The pressurization of A/C will be prevented on ground only.
c.              The pressurization of A/C has no affection.

Questions.QuestionString: 19.           The indication 'SLIDE ARMED’ at the DIP ( door indication panel )comes on when(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              A/C on ground and slide lever is armed.
b.              A/C on ground, slide lever is armed; the door operation handle lifted a few degrees.
c.              A/C on ground, slide lever is armed, parking brake is on, and the door operation handle is moved to start lifting the door.

Questions.QuestionString: 1.              The main sections of pylon(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The pylon box, pylon fairing, pylon secondary structure.
b.              The pylon box, pylon primary structure, pylon secondary structure, pylon fairing.
c.              Pylon box, pylon fairing.

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              The main functions of pylon(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Hold the power plant.
b.              Hold the power plant; transmit the engine thrust to the aircraft.
c.              Hold the power plant, transmit the engine thrust to the aircraft, and protect the ducts and wirings from engine to aircraft.

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              The engine is attached to the pylon(ME Repair)
Answers: a.              By two attachment fittings located on the lower surface of pylon box.
b.              By three attachment fittings located on the lower surface of pylon box.
c.              By two attachment fittings and a thrust link.

Questions.QuestionString: 4.              Inspection of the Pylons for missing fasteners(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              No missing fastener on the forward edge of the panels, no missing fastener in the corners.
b.              No missing fastener on the forward edge of the panels, one fastener can be missing in the corners.
c.              Dispatch A/C with defect 10 flight cycles for missing fasteners on the corners.

Questions.QuestionString: 5.              The nacelle major assemblies are(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Air inlet cowl, fan cowl doors, thrust reverser assembly, exhaust system.
b.              Thrust reverser assembly, the engine.
c.              Air inlet cowl, fan cowl doors, thrust reverser assembly, exhaust system, engine.

Questions.QuestionString: 6.              With one fan cowl latch is opened(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The fan cowl latch access panel cannot close.
b.              The closure of fan cowl latch access panel is independent on the status of fan cowl latches.
c.              Closing the fan cowl access panel will cause the closure of fan cowl latch.

Questions.QuestionString: 7.              When PDOS ( power door operating system) inoperative(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              The fan cowl and thrust reverser cowl can be opened by operating external pump that connects to opening actuator.
b.              The fan cowl is opened by pushing manually; the thrust reverser cowl can be opened by operating external pump.
c.              The fan cowl and thrust reverser cowl are opened by pushing manually

Questions.QuestionString: 8.              If fuel leak at the double-wall fuel pipe inside the pylon(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              It will be drained to engine drain mast.
b.              It will be drained to pylon drain port on the aft section of the aft fairing
c.              It will be sent to engine fuel drain collector tank.

Questions.QuestionString: 1.              The Trimmable Horizontal Stabilizer (THS) function(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              Maintain A/C stable along the pitch axis.
b.              Together with the elevators to control the pitch of the aircraft.
c.              Control the aircraft and keep it stable around its pitch axis.

Questions.QuestionString: 2.              You see some holes along the bottom surface of THS during transit check(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.          It is abnormal; you must apply hi speed tape to maintain the aerodynamic contour of the THS.
b.         Check for leak and then seal by hi speed tape.
c.          It is normal; it belongs to the THS drainage system.

Questions.QuestionString: 3.              The THS is protected against lighting strikes(ME Repair, IFE Repair)
Answers: a.              By static dischargers.
b.              By copper foil and metallic bonding strips on skin of THS.
